title: TCA Scan-Before-Connect Compliance from NAC or Agent Logs

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-010-4 R5 Part 5.3.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-010-4 R5 Part 5.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.55: TCA Scan-Before-Connect Compliance from NAC or Agent Logs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.55' running on index=network sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius or sourcetype=pan:globalprotect, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=network sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius earliest=-24h Acct-Status-Type=Start
| lookup tca_device_registry.csv calling_station_id OUTPUT asset_tag
| where isnotnull(asset_tag)
| join type=left calling_station_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype=defender:scan earliest=-24h
    | rename device_id as calling_station_id
    | stats latest(scan_result) as scan by calling_station_id
  ]
| where scan!="clean" OR isnull(scan)
| table _time, calling_station_id, user, scan, asset_tag
description: We verify transient cyber assets received a successful posture or malware scan before being granted ESP network access.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes endpoint, network and sourcetypes cisco:ise:radius, cisco:ise:radius`, defender:scan. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align RADIUS calling-station IDs with endpoint inventory keys. (2) Tune for your NAC vendor sourcetype. (3) Block or quarantine integration outside Splunk. (4) Retain pass/fail for audits. Indexes required: `index=endpoint`, `index=network`; Lookups: `tca_device_registry`, `tca_device_registry.csv`.
visualization: Table (non-compliant TCA), Single value (count), Timeline. Key fields: `asset_tag`, `calling_station_id`, `scan`, `user`.
knownFalsePositives: Patches deferred for compatibility testing, vendor lag on stable releases, or planned outage windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius` or `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align RADIUS calling-station IDs with endpoint inventory keys. (2) Tune for your NAC vendor sourcetype. (3) Block or quarantine integration outside Splunk. (4) Retain pass/fail for audits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius earliest=-24h Acct-Status-Type=Start
| lookup tca_device_registry.csv calling_station_id OUTPUT asset_tag
| where isnotnull(asset_tag)
| join type=left calling_station_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype=defender:scan earliest=-24h
    | rename device_id as calling_station_id
    | stats latest(scan_result) as scan by calling_station_id
  ]
| where scan!="clean" OR isnull(scan)
| table _time, calling_station_id, user, scan, asset_tag
```

Understanding this SPL

**TCA Scan-Before-Connect Compliance from NAC or Agent Logs (CIP-010-4 R5 Part 5.3)** — We verify transient cyber assets received a successful posture or malware scan before being granted ESP network access.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius` or `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:radius. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(asset_tag)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where scan!="clean" OR isnull(scan)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Output fields: _time, asset_tag, calling_station_id, scan, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant TCA), Single value (count), Timeline
grandmaExplanation: We verify transient cyber assets received a successful posture or malware scan before being granted ESP network access. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
